title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment
exclusions: Does NOT synthesise objectives; consumes the objectives the ISMS committee has set and reports current attainment against the numeric target for each.
evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_6_2_objective_attainment' (daily) emits per objective: objective_id, target, current, attainment_pct, trend_7d, status, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 6.2 requires information-security objectives to be measurable; live attainment against the documented target is the direct evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 6.2 (Information-security objectives) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.47: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.47' running on sourcetype isms:objective_result and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 9.1 (monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) — same measurements feed it.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.47: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An objective whose current value is below its target returns status=below_target in the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An objective meeting its target returns status=met.

dataSources: `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result (push from ISMS KPIs); objective targets lookup `iso_27001_objectives.csv`.
spl:
| inputlookup iso_27001_objectives.csv
| join type=outer objective_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(value) AS current latest(_time) AS measured_at BY objective_id
   | eval measured_at=strftime(measured_at,"%Y-%m-%d")]
| eval attainment_pct=round(100*current/target,1)
| eval status=if(attainment_pct>=100,"met","below_target")
| table objective_id target current attainment_pct measured_at status
description: Aligns every Clause 6.2 numeric objective (e.g., 'mean-time-to-patch <30d', 'phishing click rate <3%', 'privileged-session-recording coverage >95%') against its live value. The table is the quarterly Board report.
value: Replaces the manual spreadsheet most ISMS programmes use to report objective attainment. Auditors see the live measurement, the target, and the last update in a single reproducible query.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment. ISO 27001 tells us what good security management looks like, and we document that we follow it.
implementation: (1) Push each KPI to index=compliance nightly with objective_id tag; (2) maintain iso_27001_objectives.csv as the ISMS-committee-approved target list; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) status=below_target opens an ISMS action; (5) quarterly Board pack exports the table. Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=isms:objective_result`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result (push from ISMS KPIs); objective targets lookup `iso_27001_objectives.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push each KPI to index=compliance nightly with objective_id tag; (2) maintain iso_27001_objectives.csv as the ISMS-committee-approved target list; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) status=below_target opens an ISMS action; (5) quarterly Board pack exports the table.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_objectives.csv
| join type=outer objective_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(value) AS current latest(_time) AS measured_at BY objective_id
   | eval measured_at=strftime(measured_at,"%Y-%m-%d")]
| eval attainment_pct=round(100*current/target,1)
| eval status=if(attainment_pct>=100,"met","below_target")
| table objective_id target current attainment_pct measured_at status
```

Understanding this SPL

**ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment** — Replaces the manual spreadsheet most ISMS programmes use to report objective attainment. Auditors see the live measurement, the target, and the last update in a single reproducible query.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result (push from ISMS KPIs); objective targets lookup `iso_27001_objectives.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **attainment_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **status** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: attainment_pct, current, measured_at, objective_id, status, target.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bullet chart of attainment % vs target, line chart of 30-day trend per objective, single value 'objectives below target'.
visualization: Bullet chart of attainment % vs target, line chart of 30-day trend per objective, single value 'objectives below target'.
knownFalsePositives: Incidents may bunch during major events, vulnerability bulletin response, or controlled exercises.
